title: Cryopreservation of Mammalian Cells
summary: Protocol for harvesting, freezing, and long-term storage of mammalian cells in liquid nitrogen using DMSO-based freezing medium.
summary_en: Protocol for harvesting, freezing, and long-term storage of mammalian cells in liquid nitrogen using DMSO-based freezing medium.

materials.name: Freezing medium: 90% FBS + 10% DMSO (or complete medium with 10% DMSO)
Isopropanol freezing container (Mr. Frosty) or controlled-rate freezer

steps.text: Harvest cells in log-phase growth by trypsinization and neutralize with complete medium.
steps.text_en: Harvest cells in log-phase growth by trypsinization and neutralize with complete medium.
steps.verbatim: Harvest cells in log-phase growth by trypsinization and neutralize with complete medium.

steps.text: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 200 x g for 5 min to pellet the cells.
steps.text_en: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 200 x g for 5 min to pellet the cells.
steps.verbatim: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 200 x g for 5 min to pellet the cells.

steps.text: Aspirate and discard the supernatant, taking care not to disturb the pellet.
steps.text_en: Aspirate and discard the supernatant, taking care not to disturb the pellet.
hazards: taking care not to disturb the pellet
hazards_en: taking care not to disturb the pellet
steps.verbatim: Aspirate and discard the supernatant, taking care not to disturb the pellet.

steps.text: Gently resuspend the pellet in cold freezing medium to a density of 1-5 x 10^6 cells per mL. Keep the cells on ice.
steps.text_en: Gently resuspend the pellet in cold freezing medium to a density of 1-5 x 10^6 cells per mL. Keep the cells on ice.

reagents.name: cold freezing medium
reagents.condition: to a density of 1-5 x 10^6 cells per mL
reagents.condition_en: to a density of 1-5 x 10^6 cells per mL

steps.verbatim: Gently resuspend the pellet in cold freezing medium to a density of 1-5 x 10^6 cells per mL. Keep the cells on ice.

steps.text: Aliquot 1 mL of the cell suspension into each labelled cryovial. Work quickly — prolonged exposure to DMSO at room temperature is toxic to cells.
steps.text_en: Aliquot 1 mL of the cell suspension into each labelled cryovial. Work quickly — prolonged exposure to DMSO at room temperature is toxic to cells.
hazards: Work quickly — prolonged exposure to DMSO at room temperature is toxic to cells.
hazards_en: Work quickly — prolonged exposure to DMSO at room temperature is toxic to cells.
steps.verbatim: Aliquot 1 mL of the cell suspension into each labelled cryovial. Work quickly — prolonged exposure to DMSO at room temperature is toxic to cells.

steps.text: Place the cryovials into an isopropanol freezing container and transfer to a -80°C freezer overnight to achieve a controlled cooling rate of about -1°C per min.
steps.text_en: Place the cryovials into an isopropanol freezing container and transfer to a -80°C freezer overnight to achieve a controlled cooling rate of about -1°C per min.
steps.verbatim: Place the cryovials into an isopropanol freezing container and transfer to a -80°C freezer overnight to achieve a controlled cooling rate of about -1°C per min.

steps.text: The next day, transfer the frozen cryovials into the liquid nitrogen dewar (vapor phase, below -150°C) for long-term storage.
steps.text_en: The next day, transfer the frozen cryovials into the liquid nitrogen dewar (vapor phase, below -150°C) for long-term storage.
hazards: Liquid nitrogen: vapor phase below -150°C, handle with appropriate cryogenic protection
hazards_en: Liquid nitrogen: vapor phase below -150°C, handle with appropriate cryogenic protection
steps.verbatim: The next day, transfer the frozen cryovials into the liquid nitrogen dewar (vapor phase, below -150°C) for long-term storage.

open_parameters.question: What cell density within the 1-5 x 10^6 cells per mL range should be targeted?
Use isopropanol freezing container (Mr. Frosty) or a controlled-rate freezer?
